창세기 21:1.content: Yahweh visited Sarah as he had said, and Yahweh did to Sarah as he had spoken.

창세기 21:2.content: Sarah conceived, and bore Abraham a son in his old age, at the set time of which God had spoken to him.

창세기 21:3.content: Abraham called his son who was born to him, whom Sarah bore to him, Isaac.

창세기 21:4.content: Abraham circumcised his son, Isaac, when he was eight days old, as God had commanded him.

창세기 21:5.content: Abraham was one hundred years old when his son, Isaac, was born to him.

창세기 21:6.content: Sarah said, “God has made me laugh. Everyone who hears will laugh with me.”

창세기 21:7.content: She said, “Who would have said to Abraham that Sarah would nurse children? For I have borne him a son in his old age.”

창세기 21:8.content: The child grew and was weaned. Abraham made a great feast on the day that Isaac was weaned.

창세기 21:9.content: Sarah saw the son of Hagar the Egyptian, whom she had borne to Abraham, mocking.

창세기 21:10.content: Therefore she said to Abraham, “Cast out this servant and her son! For the son of this servant will not be heir with my son, Isaac.”

창세기 21:11.content: The thing was very grievous in Abraham’s sight on account of his son.

창세기 21:12.content: God said to Abraham, “Don’t let it be grievous in your sight because of the boy, and because of your servant. In all that Sarah says to you, listen to her voice. For your offspring will be named through Isaac.

창세기 21:13.content: I will also make a nation of the son of the servant, because he is your child.”

창세기 21:14.content: Abraham rose up early in the morning, and took bread and a container of water, and gave it to Hagar, putting it on her shoulder; and gave her the child, and sent her away. She departed, and wandered in the wilderness of Beersheba.

창세기 21:15.content: The water in the container was spent, and she put the child under one of the shrubs.

창세기 21:16.content: She went and sat down opposite him, a good way off, about a bow shot away. For she said, “Don’t let me see the death of the child.” She sat opposite him, and lifted up her voice, and wept.

창세기 21:17.content: God heard the voice of the boy. The angel of God called to Hagar out of the sky, and said to her, “What troubles you, Hagar? Don’t be afraid. For God has heard the voice of the boy where he is.

창세기 21:18.content: Get up, lift up the boy, and hold him with your hand. For I will make him a great nation.”

창세기 21:19.content: God opened her eyes, and she saw a well of water. She went, filled the container with water, and gave the boy a drink.

창세기 21:20.content: God was with the boy, and he grew. He lived in the wilderness, and as he grew up, became an archer.

창세기 21:21.content: He lived in the wilderness of Paran. His mother got a wife for him out of the land of Egypt.

창세기 21:22.content: At that time, Abimelech and Phicol the captain of his army spoke to Abraham, saying, “God is with you in all that you do.

창세기 21:23.content: Now, therefore, swear to me here by God that you will not deal falsely with me, nor with my son, nor with my son’s son. But according to the kindness that I have done to you, you shall do to me, and to the land in which you have lived as a foreigner.”

창세기 21:25.content: Abraham complained to Abimelech because of a water well, which Abimelech’s servants had violently taken away.

창세기 21:26.content: Abimelech said, “I don’t know who has done this thing. You didn’t tell me, and I didn’t hear of it until today.”

창세기 21:27.content: Abraham took sheep and cattle, and gave them to Abimelech. Those two made a covenant.

창세기 21:28.content: Abraham set seven ewe lambs of the flock by themselves.

창세기 21:29.content: Abimelech said to Abraham, “What do these seven ewe lambs, which you have set by themselves, mean?”

창세기 21:30.content: He said, “You shall take these seven ewe lambs from my hand, that it may be a witness to me, that I have dug this well.”

창세기 21:31.content: Therefore he called that place Beersheba, because they both swore an oath there.

창세기 21:32.content: So they made a covenant at Beersheba. Abimelech rose up with Phicol, the captain of his army, and they returned into the land of the Philistines.

창세기 21:33.content: Abraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beersheba, and called there on the name of Yahweh, the Everlasting God.

창세기 21:34.content: Abraham lived as a foreigner in the land of the Philistines many days.
